text: A convolutional filter slides over an image to extract features. Watch how the 3 by 3 red filter moves across the 5 by 5 blue input image.
At each position, the filter computes a dot product: multiplying overlapping values and summing them up. The result goes into the green feature map.
When the filter is here, we multiply one by one, two by zero, three by zero, and continue through all nine overlapping cells, adding them together.
The filter slides right, then down, systematically covering the entire image. Each output captures local patterns from its receptive field.
Convolution transforms image data into feature representations, enabling neural networks to detect edges, textures, and complex patterns.
